title: Menu Properties
text: Menu Properties Menu properties enable you to control the appearance of menus using the font, colors, and images. They are applicable only for Windows applications and are defined by the menu logical in the [application] section of the application initialization file ( usys.ini ). You can set the default colors to use for the background and text, as well as other colors to indicate which item is selected, or items that are disabled in the current context. When a color property is not defined, the Windows default is used. Table 1. Menu properties Property Description Details Font Font used for text in menus. Font Color Properties BackColor Background color of drop-down menu and popup menus. Background Color (BackColor) BackColorSelect Background color of the selected menu item. Active Background Color (BackColorSelect) BackColorSidebar Background color of the sidebar in drop-down and popup menus. BackColorSidebar BackColorFill Style of the background color for the selected menu item: flat or gradient . Gradient Fill Style (BackColorFill) BorderColorSelect Color of the border around a selected menu item. BorderColorSelect ForeColor Color of the text in menu items and separators in menus. Foreground Color (ForeColor) ForeColorSelect Color of the text when the menu item is selected. Active Foreground Color (ForecolorSelect) ForeColorDisabled Color of the text when the menu item is disabled. ForeColorDisabled Image Properties ImgSelect Image displayed as the selected menu item. Active Image (ImgSelect) ImgSidebar Image displayed as the side bar of a drop-down or popup menu. Overrides the BackColorSidebar property. ImgSidebar ImgSize Size used to display an image in front of the menu item. Uniface always reserves an area in front of a menu item for an image. Image Size (ImgSize) Important: Using the Menu setting bypasses the Windows theme manager, so you need to explicitly specify all the properties you want to control. For each color property that is not defined in the property string, the system-defined color is used, which gives menus a flat appearance. You can use the BackColorFill property to set the default to a gradient fill. Example: Setting Menu Properties The following example sets the font, foreground, background, and selection colors for menus. (For readability, this example is formatted over several lines.) menu=umenu(font=SansMedium; backcolor=#fdfdfd;forecolor=black;forecolorselect=green; backcolorselect=#c0ddfc;bordercolorselect=#3399ff) Copy Figure 1. Result Highlighting a Selection You can use BackColorSelect to specify a color or ImgSelect to specify an image to highlight the item that has focus. Tip: When defining the appearance of menus, keep in mind that colors have a performance advantage over images. If you specify a small image, it is automatically stretched to fit the width of the menu. For example, the image yellow.png is very small: menu=umenu(font=SansMedium;imgselect=@yellow.png) Copy In the displayed menu, it is expanded. Figure 2. Menu with ImgSelect Property Menu Sidebar By default, menus in Windows have a sidebar to the left of the menu items (or to the right if the application is configure for a right-to-left locale). This space is reserved to display an image or checkmark associated with the menu item. A separate color or image can be defined or the sidebar. You can use the BackColorSidebar to define an alternative color for this sidebar, or the ImgSidebar to specify an image. menu=umenu(font=SansMedium;imgsidebar=@bluevertical.png) Copy Figure 3. Menu with Images for Sidebar and Selection Related Links Menu Set the font, color, and image properties for menus. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
